ref: Noah did everything God told him to do.
text: Nowa yaa nye dii meena ti Wen weeni wa la.
translated: Nowa is the one who gave me the money to go to Wen.

ref: But God answered: No! You and Sarah will have a son. His name will be Isaac, and I will make an everlasting promise to him and his descendants.
text: Wen yaa weeni Abrayam ayen, m siak ya, alege fi powa Saara ale biak nidoabili ate fi te wa yuei ayen Aizik. M le basi ate mi ale wa nɔiyeŋka a zaani ate ka chim nɔiyeŋ kaai ale kaa kpeglim a te wa ŋaaŋbisaŋa ale bo wa ŋaaŋ cheen la.
translated: When Abraham was old, he gave all he had to Isaac. He also gave gifts to the sons of his concubines and sent them away from his son Isaac, to the east country.

ref: Jacob cut branches from some poplar trees and from some almond and evergreen trees. He peeled off part of the bark and made the branches look spotted and speckled.

translated: Jacob went to the market to buy almonds and peanuts and peanuts, but when he arrived, he saw that the prices were high, so he decided to buy only a small amount.

ref: As Jacob was on his way back home, some of God's angels came and met him.
text: Jekob mɛ yaa lɔ wa suik po a cheŋ, ate Wen maarɔma yaa tu wa suik.

ref: So God created humans to be like himself; he made men and women.
